commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Do not queue repair while the only blocker is contributor real-behavior proof plus normal maintainer review of the current PR branch.; Cleared: The diff changes CLI/operator message rendering, tests, and one pre-commit dependency specifier without adding new dependency sources, permissions, secrets handling, or code-execution paths. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds node-host recovery guidance to the shared exec approval-unavailable renderer, propagates host/node metadata through deterministic tool-result output, adds a renderer assertion, and fixes the skills pre-commit pytest dependency specifier.

PR surface: Source +30, Tests +12, Other 0. Total +42 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main routes node-host approval-unavailable output through the shared renderer, and that renderer lacks the concrete dashboard and node approvals commands. I did not run a live approval timeout or denial flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Do not queue repair while the only blocker is contributor real-behavior proof plus normal maintainer review of the current PR branch.

target.title: fix(cli): add exec approval recovery guidance (Fixes #53250)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #53250.

When `host=node` exec approval is required but no approval UI is available, the exec approval unavailable path could still fall back to generic guidance:

`exec denied: approval required (approval UI not available)`

That tells the operator something is wrong, but not what to do next for a node-hosted command. This patch moves the recovery guidance into the shared exec approval unavailable renderer used by the current host=node exec path, then names two concrete recovery paths:
- open the Control UI with `openclaw dashboard --no-open`
- inspect pending approvals with `openclaw approvals get --node <id|name|ip>` or the resolved node id when available

## Tests and validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-shared.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts
git diff --check
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:
- the node exec approval unavailable message should include actionable next steps instead of dead-end generic guidance

Environment tested:
- local source checkout on this branch

Evidence after the fix:
- `buildExecApprovalUnavailableReplyPayload({ reason: "no-approval-route", host: "node", nodeId: "mac-1" })` now renders both `openclaw dashboard --no-open` and `openclaw approvals get --node mac-1`
- the host/node metadata is passed from the exec host result and preserved by the deterministic tool-result output path

Observed result:
- users hitting the current shared host=node unavailable path get concrete approval inbox commands in the message itself

What was not tested:
- a live node exec approval timeout/denial flow

## Risk

Low. This is a renderer-only guidance improvement with no behavior change in the approval flow itself.
target.author: deepujain
